question: Can Ethereum block builders be clustered into behaviorally distinct groups using observable block-construction traits from Xatu relay delivery and bid trace data?
hypothesis: Builders exhibit stable, distinguishable block-construction styles that can be characterized along observable dimensions: relay routing preference, block gas utilization, delivery frequency, bid intensity, and value distribution. These traits cluster into a small number of behavioral archetypes that go beyond simple market share ranking.

decision.rationale: The slice establishes an 8-dimensional fingerprinting framework for Ethereum block builders using Xatu relay delivery and bid trace data. The framework identifies structural feasibility of behavioral clustering into 4-5 archetypes and documents testable correlation hypotheses. Confidence is medium due to aggregate-level analysis without per-builder query execution and the 18-month date mismatch between data sources.

tables_used.name: mev_relay_proposer_payload_delivered
tables_used.notes: 6,597 unique delivered blocks, 47 builders, 8 relays; supports 6 fingerprint dimensions (market share, relay routing, value distribution, gas utilization)

tables_used.name: mev_relay_bid_trace
tables_used.notes: 6,620 slots, 109 builders; supports 2 additional fingerprint dimensions (bid intensity, num_tx per bid); 18-month date gap from delivery data

reproducibility.data_source: Xatu public parquet export via ethpandaops
reproducibility.notes: Structural feasibility analysis for builder behavioral fingerprinting. Row counts from prior scoping. Per-builder GROUP BY queries deferred to follow-on execution slice.
